_meta.purpose: Tag products with 'Loading Docks' (and supporting 'Service yard and dock protection') in their fitForPurposeApplications where the original PRH-1009 entry omitted the explicit dock label. Authority: ASAFE_GroundWorks_PRH-1005 (which lists the dock-gate family with a 'dock-door zones' substrate note) and the ASAFE_ProductSuitability_PRH-1009 inclusion criteria for HGV-class capable barriers.
_meta.updateNote: May 6 2026 — Sagarika reported that Loading Docks recommendations were surfacing iFlex Double Traffic / Plus, which is wrong: the iFlex Double Traffic family is a warehouse traffic-pedestrian barrier (5.4-12kJ class) not a HGV-class dock-perimeter solution. Removed both from the 'Loading Docks' tag (kept on 'Service yard and dock protection' which IS appropriate). Atlas-class barriers stay on the dock list because Atlas Double Traffic is the 35kJ HGV-rated equivalent that A-SAFE actually deploys at dock perimeters.
_meta.policy: Conservative interpretation v2: only HGV-class barriers (Atlas family, dedicated dock products, height restrictors at dock doors, bollards at dock corners, vehicle stops at dock-leveller edges, topple barriers at staging) get the 'Loading Docks' tag. iFlex Double Traffic / Plus stays on 'Service yard' (yard perimeter) but NOT on 'Loading Docks' (dock face).

additions.comment: 35kJ HGV-class heavy-duty barrier — A-SAFE's canonical recommendation for dock-perimeter traffic where loaded HGVs / heavy forklifts approach loading bays. Replaced the over-promoted iFlex Double Traffic in the May-6 recommender correction.
Atlas family with elevated upper rail — same dock-perimeter application as Atlas Double Traffic, used where pedestrian visibility above the lower rail is required.

Service-yard rated only — dropped from 'Loading Docks' May-6 2026 after rep feedback that it was being over-recommended at HGV-class dock faces. Atlas is the right product at the dock leveller; this stays for the broader yard perimeter.
Service-yard rated only — see iFlex Double Traffic+ note above.
Service-yard rated only — counterbalance-FLT class is below the HGV-class threshold A-SAFE uses for the dock-face itself.
Heavy Duty Topple Barrier family — explicitly rated for Heavy Duty Counterbalance Truck class; deployed at dock-edge approach to prevent topple at staging.
Same Heavy Duty Topple family; long span suits the dock-door perimeter.
Industrial door protection for HGV-class vehicles entering dock buildings — direct dock-door application.
Companion post for the height restrictor; same dock-door deployment.
PRH-1009 already lists 'Industrial door protection' + 'Goods In Goods Out' — these are loading-dock applications by definition.
Service-yard category vehicle stop; coach terminals are a dock-class loading environment.
Service-yard category vehicle stop; appropriate for car-class dock entries (parking-deck loading bays).
190mm OD bollard — same form factor as iFlex HD Bollard which already carries 'Service yard and dock protection'. Used for dock-perimeter pillar protection.
Cold-storage-rated 190mm bollard for cold-store dock entries (food/pharma temperature-controlled docks).
Pedestrian + traffic segregation family deployed alongside dock-door staging when foot traffic mingles with HGV/FLT.
Heavy-Duty Counterbalance Truck rated; suits internal goods-handling dock approaches where larger micro-barrier coverage is needed.
